figures.caption: FIG. 1. Application of Blochs theorem and associated downfolding exemplified by hexagonal MnI2. (a) crystal structure, (b) band structure in super cell with energy relative to the Fermi-energy, (c) band structure in primitive cell with GBT with energy relative to the Fermi-energy, (d) magnetic moments in super cell and primitive cell, (e) spin expectation value within the magnetic Brillouin zone, and (f) spin expectation value within the crystallographic Brillouin zone. The bands chosen in (e) and (f) are indicated by arrows in (b) and (c).
FIG. 3. MnTe2 Fermi surface colored according to spin expectation value. The calculation is made for the super cell corresponding to Q = (1/3, 1/3).
FIG. 2. Projected p-orbital character of magnetic ions (Mn). The p-wave character is averaged over the three Mn atoms in the super cell.
